description: Optional SessionEnd hook that prints a reminder to run /retro after non-trivial sessions. Off by default — copy to your ~/.claude/hooks/ or <project>/.claude/hooks/ to enable.

hooks.command: bash -c 'tp=$(jq -r ".transcript_path // empty" 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$tp" ] || [ ! -r "$tp" ] && exit 0; tokens=$(wc -w < "$tp" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); if [ "${tokens:-0}" -gt 1000 ]; then printf "Session was non-trivial (%s words). Run /retro to extract learnings.\n" "$tokens"; fi'

notes.input: SessionEnd hook input is JSON on stdin with a 'transcript_path' field (NOT $CLAUDE_TRANSCRIPT_PATH env var). This command reads stdin and extracts transcript_path via jq.
notes.enable-instructions: This hook only prints a reminder. To actually invoke /retro automatically, replace the command with a way to invoke the slash command in your client. For Claude Code CLI, this is currently a manual user action — the hook serves as a nudge.
notes.off-by-default-rationale: Auto-running /retro on every session end would burn tokens for trivial sessions. The user opts in by copying this file.
notes.dependencies: Requires jq (for stdin JSON parsing). The hook silently exits if jq is unavailable or transcript_path is empty/unreadable.
